qa_pairs.question: How do users obtain access to the Trillium cluster, including its CPU, GPU, and HPSS components?
qa_pairs.answer: Users must opt-in for the Trillium cluster via the CCDB webpage at https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/me/access_systems. Users who had opted into Niagara or Mist, or HPSS through Niagara, before August 5, 2025, will be automatically opted-in. Opting into Trillium grants access to both its CPU and GPU subclusters, while HPSS requires a separate opt-in on the same CCDB page.

qa_pairs.question: How do users log into the Trillium CPU and GPU subclusters using a terminal, and what essential command should be run after the first login?
qa_pairs.answer: To log into the Trillium CPU subcluster, users should SSH to `USERNAME@trillium.alliancecan.ca`. For the Trillium GPU subcluster, users should SSH to `USERNAME@trillium-gpu.alliancecan.ca`. Both login methods require the use of SSH keys and Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) enabled on your CCDB account. After the first successful login, it is highly recommended to run the command `trisetup` to set up initial configuration files like `.bashrc` and create important directories and symbolic links to your scratch and project directories.

qa_pairs.question: Could you describe the hardware specifications for the compute nodes in both the CPU and GPU subclusters of the Trillium cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Each compute node in the Trillium CPU subcluster features 192 AMD Zen 5 (Turin) cores and 755 GB of available memory, with a total of 1224 such nodes. For the GPU subcluster, each compute node is equipped with 96 AMD Zen 4 (Genoa) cores, 755 GB of available memory, and 4 NVIDIA H100 GPUs. There are 61 GPU compute nodes in total.
